---
title: "Cover Letter"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    linestretch: 1.15
    number-sections: false
    toc: false
  docx:
    toc: false
---

\today

Dear Editor,

We are pleased to submit our manuscript entitled **"Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis in Adults: A Systematic Review of Pathogenesis, Diagnosis, and Management (2016--2026)"** for consideration for publication in your journal.

Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) is a life-threatening hyperinflammatory syndrome with mortality rates exceeding 50% in untreated adults. The past decade has witnessed transformative advances in our understanding and management of this condition, driven by: (1) the publication of the first adult-specific management guidelines; (2) the approval of emapalumab as the first targeted therapy for HLH; (3) the emergence of iatrogenic triggers from CAR T-cell therapy and immune checkpoint inhibitors; and (4) the COVID-19 pandemic, which brought cytokine storm syndromes to global clinical attention.

Despite these advances, no comprehensive systematic review has synthesized the full scope of developments across pathogenesis, diagnostic criteria, etiological classification, treatment strategies, and the COVID-19 paradigm in a single, integrated article. Our review addresses this gap by systematically searching Scopus, PubMed, and Embase for publications from 2016 to 2026, applying rigorous quality filtering (CiteScore $\geq$ 3.0), and validating every DOI to ensure reference integrity. From 897 initial records, 50 high-impact articles across 36 journals were selected for narrative thematic synthesis.

**Key contributions of this review include:**

- A comprehensive synthesis of the IFN-$\gamma$--macrophage activation axis as the validated central pathogenic pathway, supported by the clinical success of emapalumab (63% response rate, $P$ = 0.02)
- Critical evaluation of diagnostic tools for adult populations: HLH-2004, the updated HLH-2024 criteria, HScore (93% sensitivity, 86% specificity at cutoff 168), and EULAR/ACR MAS criteria
- The first systematic cataloging of iatrogenic HLH triggers, including the distinction between cytokine release syndrome and immune effector cell-associated HLH-like syndrome (IEC-HS) in the CAR T-cell setting
- An evidence-based treatment framework encompassing etoposide-based protocols with adult dose modifications, targeted therapies (emapalumab, anakinra, ruxolitinib, tocilizumab), and trigger-specific strategies
- A nuanced analysis of the COVID-19 and HLH overlap, distinguishing shared pathophysiology from critical immunological differences

This review was conducted in full compliance with the PRISMA 2020 guidelines, with a completed 27-item checklist provided as an appendix. All references have been validated through DOI resolution, and the complete search methodology, data, and analytic materials are publicly available.

We believe this manuscript will be of significant interest to hematologists, intensivists, rheumatologists, and oncologists who encounter HLH in clinical practice, and that it provides a timely, comprehensive resource as the therapeutic landscape continues to evolve.

This manuscript has not been published previously and is not under consideration for publication elsewhere. All authors have approved the submitted version.

Thank you for your consideration. We look forward to hearing from you.

Sincerely,

Hsieh-Ting Lin\
Department of Hematology\
ORCID: 0009-0002-3974-4528
